tossups.question: Note to players: Description acceptable. This person is the subject of a portrait in profile in which they sit illuminated against an open door while several trees blow in the wind.An artist who set up his studio in an upstairs room of this person’s house painted fluttering window curtains in the work Wind from the Sea. In a late painting, this person is represented by the dilapidated blue door of a kitchen, while a shadowy brown door represents her brother Alvaro. Betsy James modelled for the most famous painting of this resident of (*) Cushing, Maine, who is thought to have suffered from Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease. In that painting, this person wears a pink gown and gazes up and right towards a farmhouse while lying in a brown field. For 10 points, name this American muse whose ‘World’ was painted by Andrew Wyeth.
tossups.question_sanitized: Note to players: Description acceptable. This person is the subject of a portrait in profile in which they sit illuminated against an open door while several trees blow in the wind.An artist who set up his studio in an upstairs room of this person's house painted fluttering window curtains in the work Wind from the Sea. In a late painting, this person is represented by the dilapidated blue door of a kitchen, while a shadowy brown door represents her brother Alvaro. Betsy James modelled for the most famous painting of this resident of (*) Cushing, Maine, who is thought to have suffered from Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease. In that painting, this person wears a pink gown and gazes up and right towards a farmhouse while lying in a brown field. For 10 points, name this American muse whose 'World' was painted by Andrew Wyeth.
tossups.answer: Anna Christina Olson [or Christina Olson; accept the person from Christina’s World, accept Anna Christina]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Anna Christina Olson [or Christina Olson; accept the person from Christina's World, accept Anna Christina]

tossups.question: This painting was reimagined for a 2015 Greenpeace campaign by the collective Kennardphillipps, which depicts its setting in the aftermath of an oil spill. This painting depicts an “extraordinary conquest of a life which most people would consider hopeless” according to its artist, who was buried in this painting’s real-life setting. Despite being 30 years older than the intended subject, the artist’s wife Betsy James modeled the upper body of this painting’s central figure, who may have suffered from Charcot–Marie–Tooth disease. In this painting set in Cushing, Maine, the title person looks towards the Olson House, which sits atop a hill in the distance. For 10 points, name this painting of a woman in a pink dress crawling in a field by Andrew Wyeth.

In this painting set in Cushing, Maine, the title person looks towards the Olson House, which sits atop a hill in the distance. For 10 points, name this painting of a woman in a pink dress crawling in a field by Andrew Wyeth.

tossups.question: The artist of this painting replaced his original muse for the work with his wife, who was 30 years the muse’s junior. It is now believed that the muse, Anna Olson, suffered from Charcot-Marie Tooth Disease, which causes muscle weakness in the legs. (*) Andrew Wyeth had great attention to detail, painting every curved blade of grass and even detailing the farmhouse in the background. The farmhouse is based on the actual Olson farmhouse near Wyeth’s vacation home in South Cushing, Maine. For ten points, name this painting that depicts a brunette in a pink dress lying in a golden field crawling and reaching for a farmhouse.
tossups.question_sanitized: The artist of this painting replaced his original muse for the work with his wife, who was 30 years the muse's junior. It is now believed that the muse, Anna Olson, suffered from Charcot-Marie Tooth Disease, which causes muscle weakness in the legs. (*) Andrew Wyeth had great attention to detail, painting every curved blade of grass and even detailing the farmhouse in the background. The farmhouse is based on the actual Olson farmhouse near Wyeth's vacation home in South Cushing, Maine. For ten points, name this painting that depicts a brunette in a pink dress lying in a golden field crawling and reaching for a farmhouse.

tossups.question: Richard Prince displayed this work next to a picture of his girlfriend playing dead in a location similar to this painting’s setting, which apparently aroused the anger of a woman dressed like a pilgrim. The artist of this work met its model while picking blueberries, and he chose to be buried in this painting’s real-life setting. This work was followed by a painting of brown and blue doors representing the subject and her brother Alvaro after their death. This painting’s artist created repeated images of the subject’s house, including “Kitchen at (*) Olson’s.” Two faint tracks are visible in this painting, which was set in Cushing, Maine. Some barns and a house are in the background of this painting, whose subjects wears a pink dress and twists awkwardly around. For 10 points, name this Andrew Wyeth painting of a paralyzed woman in a field.

This work was followed by a painting of brown and blue doors representing the subject and her brother Alvaro after their death. Some barns and a house are in the background of this painting, whose subjects wears a pink dress and twists awkwardly around. For 10 points, name this Andrew Wyeth painting of a paralyzed woman in a field.

bonuses.leadin: Several Cézanne-esque paintings of this state, such as The Ice Hole, were created by an artist who moved back to this state due to World War I. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this state, the frequent subject of paintings by Marsden Hartley. The title girl of another painting set in this state looks up at the Olson House.

Before returning to the US, Hartley depicted several military decorations in his abstract portrait of an officer from this country. One of this country’s leaders labeled its New Objectivity movement as “degenerate art.”

The genesis of this Precisionist painting supposedly occurred when an author waiting for Hartley to answer his door watched traffic rush by in the rain outside, inspiring a poem that led a different artist to create this painting.
answers: Germany [or Deutschland; accept German Empire or Deutsches Reich; accept Weimar Republic; accept Nazi Germany]
I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold (by Charles Demuth)
answers_sanitized: Maine (The second painting is Christina's World.)
Germany [or Deutschland; accept German Empire or Deutsches Reich; accept Weimar Republic; accept Nazi Germany]
I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold (by Charles Demuth)

bonuses.leadin: After his father died in a car crash, an artist with this surname painted a boy running down a hill in front of the crash site. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this surname. The massive painting Portrait of Pig is by an artist with this surname, who painted a striking self-portrait of himself with a pumpkin for a head.

The composition of the aforementioned painting, Winter, 1946, is similar to another Andrew Wyeth painting titled for this woman’s "World," in which she looks up a field toward a house. Her common first name is acceptable.

The hill depicted in Christina's World is in this US state. Winslow Homer painted many of his sea paintings while at Prouts Neck on the coast of this state, which Marsden Hartley dubbed himself the “painter of.”
answers: Wyeth [accept Andrew Wyeth or Andrew Newell Wyeth; accept Jamie Wyeth or Jamie Browning Wyeth]
Christina [or Anna Christina Olson or Christina Olson; accept Christina’s World]

packet.name: M - Liberty B, Northwestern A, NYU A, Warwick A, Waterloo A

answers_sanitized: Wyeth [accept Andrew Wyeth or Andrew Newell Wyeth; accept Jamie Wyeth or Jamie Browning Wyeth]
Christina [or Anna Christina Olson or Christina Olson; accept Christina's World]

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer some questions about notable depictions of illness in visual art.
parts: This triptych was painted for a German monastery which specialized in caring for people with skin diseases. Painter Matthias Grunewald depicted Jesus with sores similar to those of the afflicted, emphasizing their shared pain.

This figure is often described as having polio, but accounts from her family indicate that her condition developed gradually since her birth. She has more recently been suggested to have Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease, a genetic condition which damages the peripheral nervous system.

This female photographer shot a picture of activist David Kirby surrounded by family on his deathbed, titled “The Face of AIDS.” Published in TIME magazine in 1990, it is considered an important milestone in public perception of the disease.
answers_sanitized: Christina from Christina's World [accept Christina Olson]
